title: Could Patriots reunite with Stefon Diggs? One Patriots writer doesn’t expect it.
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Stefon Diggs, a free agent after one season with the Patriots, has hinted interest in joining a new NFL team.
Patriots reporter Phil Perry is doubtful that Diggs will return to New England, despite his production in 2025.
New England's focus on adding A.J. Brown and signing Romeo Doubs decreases chances for a Diggs reunion.

title: Man, 31, killed in New Bedford shooting
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Steven Coan, 31, was found with multiple gunshot wounds in his car on Russell Street, New Bedford.
Police responded to gunshots detected by ShotSpotter near Acushnet Avenue and Russell Street on May 6.
The incident is under investigation as a homicide by State Police and New Bedford police.

title: Hayden, Rollins, and third Suffolk DA candidate Champion secure enough signatures to appear on ballot
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Kevin Hayden, Rachael Rollins, and Linda Champion qualify for Suffolk DA primary after collecting over 1,000 signatures each.
Primary candidates are Hayden with 1,249 Boston signatures, Champion with 1,349, and Rollins with 1,430, among others from three communities.
The Suffolk DA primary on September 1 will reduce the candidates to two for the November general election.

title: These are Boston’s highest-paid tech CEOs, BBJ data shows
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Wayfair's CEO Niraj Shah topped Boston's highest-paid tech CEOs with $280.9 million, a 990% increase from 2024.
Vincent Roche of Analog Devices followed with $27.5 million, while Neil Barua of PTC earned $24.3 million in 2025.
The ranking used stock awards, bonuses, and salary data from U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission proxy statements.

title: Karen Read’s SUV heads to auction in Woburn. Here’s what readers think it’s worth.
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: The 2021 Lexus LX 570 associated with Karen Read's trial is up for auction in Woburn, Massachusetts.
Boston.com readers predict the SUV could sell from below $70,000 to over $500,000, depending on its notoriety.
The SUV has been repaired but previously had issues like a dead battery and mouse infestation, affecting its value.

title: Shooting on Memorial Drive in Cambridge leaves ‘at least’ 1 wounded, police say
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: A police-involved shooting on Memorial Drive in Cambridge wounded at least one person, Massachusetts State Police reported.
The suspect, who allegedly fired shots near River Street intersection, is in custody; no ongoing public threat, authorities confirmed.
Cambridge police advised residents to avoid the area as they continue their investigation of the shooting incident.

title: Police investigating viral video of apparent food contamination at McDonald’s in Mass.
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Southbridge police are investigating a viral video showing alleged food contamination at a local McDonald's.
The video shows an employee eating fries before placing them in a to-go container, sparking public health concerns.
The involved employees have left McDonald’s, and authorities found no public health violations after their investigation.

title: MassINC poll: Many Bay Staters say their quality of life is slipping in Mass.
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: MassINC poll finds 40% of Massachusetts residents say their quality of life has declined over the past five years.
More than half of residents view housing as unaffordable, with this number rising to 65% for those aged 35 to 44.
Only 27% of respondents expect financial improvement in the coming year, reflecting economic pessimism in the region.

title: Celtics reportedly tried to land Giannis Antetokounmpo at the trade deadline
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Celtics were among several teams pursuing Giannis Antetokounmpo at the February trade deadline, per ESPN's Shams Charania.
Acquiring Antetokounmpo would require Boston to move significant salary and draft assets due to his $58 million contract.
Antetokounmpo, two-time MVP, considering options after Bucks' recent playoff struggles and is open to being traded.

title: Student charged in Danvers home invasion murder appears in court
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Anthony DeMayo, 18, charged with murder and home invasion, pleads not guilty in Essex Superior Court via video from Bridgewater State Hospital.
Police arrested DeMayo in March after Janet Swallow, 68, was found dead in her Danvers home; he was found with a blood-stained knife.
Judge granted a request for an independent competency evaluation following an earlier ruling declaring DeMayo incompetent to stand trial.
